## Page 355 — Writing

**Writing Task**

Read the stimulus material below, then complete the writing task.

**Stimulus:**

"The most important skill a student can develop is not found in any textbook. It is the ability to keep trying when things become difficult."

**Writing Prompt:**

Write a persuasive essay in response to this statement. In your essay:
- State your position clearly
- Provide reasons and examples to support your argument
- Consider an opposing viewpoint and address it
- Write in clear, well-structured paragraphs

**You will be assessed on:**
- The clarity and strength of your argument
- Your use of evidence and examples
- The structure and organisation of your writing
- Vocabulary and sentence variety
- Grammar, punctuation and spelling

**Time allowed:** 25 minutes
**Suggested length:** 350–500 words

---

**Planning Space:**

Use this space to plan your response before writing. Planning is not assessed.
